title: L_Ender 's Cataclysm Delight
description: Adds 50+ dishes, linking L_Ender's Cataclysm and Farmer's Delight in a Vanilla style.
body:
![](https://i.imgur.com/RMjVmcY.png)

## 📜 Essence This mod adds 50+ new food items and recipes, bringing L_Ender’s Cataclysm and Farmer’s Delight together in a way that feels natural and immersive. It keeps the vanilla vibe but expands the cooking system with Cataclysm’s unique ingredients. ## 🎯 Features - 50+ new food items inspired by Cataclysm’s creatures and lore. - Full Farmer’s Delight support: cutting, cooking, crafting. - Balanced food effects that fit vanilla survival. - Exotic ingredients like Amethyst Crab Meat, Deepling Meat, Coral Chunks. - Dishes range from simple snacks like Lionfish Roll to big meals like Cooked Leviathan. - Adds a set of powerful knives: Abyssal Knife, Ignitium Knife, and more. - Introduced a custom system for player stat progression — no resets just steady growth. ## 📝 Description Lender’s Delight blends L_Ender’s Cataclysm and Farmer’s Delight into one experience, adding 50+ new food items. I wanted to make the strange and dangerous world of Cataclysm useful in a new way — through cooking. ![](https://i.imgur.com/mWlOhOk.png) ![](https://i.imgur.com/LK83LJ6.png) You’ll find ingredients like Amethyst Crab Meat and Coral Golem Meat, which can be turned into snacks like the Berserker Stick or full meals like Cooked Leviathan. Some recipes are simple — Lionfish Roll or Coral Chunk Sandwich — while others push deeper, like the Maledictus Heart Stew or Ender Blood Glazed Maledictus Heart (yeah, that one sounds scary but it's worth a try). There’s also fun stuff like Void Popsicles and Crystallized Coral Pie, which you can actually slice and serve. And for players who like tools: the mod adds a full set of knives, from the sleek Abyssal Knife to the powerful Witherite Knife — all designed to work smoothly with Farmer’s Delight mechanics. ### Stat Growth System A brand-new Stat System has landed in L_Ender's Cataclysm Delight. It lets players gain permanent, stackable bonuses by eating powerful food. No UI, no grind — just growth, one bite at a time. When eating special meals, you’ll now see a tooltip preview of what each dish boosts. These are the stats you can level up: - Armor Boost — Adds bonus protection on top of what your armor already gives. - Attack Boost — Think Strength potion, but baked into your character. - Speed Boost — Run faster. - Jump Boost — Higher leaps, percentage-based scaling. - Attack Speed — Reduced cooldown between hits — snappier combat. - Crit Chance — Makes even normal hits potentially critical. Cap at 100% = every hit crits. - Knockback Resistance — Enhances armor that already reduces knockback (like Netherite). - Passive Regen — Heal 2 HP every 5 seconds at 100%. Scales by percent, so 1% = once every 500s. - Swim Speed — Makes movement underwater way faster. > All stat progress persists after death — no resets. ## 🙏 Credits - Huge thanks to the [L_Ender’s Cataclysm](https://modrinth.com/mod/l_enders-cataclysm) team for creating such a rich and inspiring world — without their work, this mod wouldn’t exist. - Big respect to the [Farmer’s Delight](https://modrinth.com/mod/farmers-delight) creators for making food mechanics fun and detailed. - Inspired by the legendary [GokiStats](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/gokistats) mod by [lacuda55](https://www.curseforge.com/members/lacuda55/projects) (big thanks to the original author). This system is fully original in both code and textures — it simply shares the spirit of progression. - The incredibly talented artist BF_Meow, who elevated the previous textures a hundredfold. Huge gratitude for work and creativity 💜 - Special thanks to Ext_Ramen496, molishang123, BF_Meow, ウーロンコヤンイ for the Chinese language support ❤️ - Shoutout to all community who helped me catch bugs. ## 🗒️ Notes - The idea for this mod came pretty simple — there’s that really annoying mob (especially early game) called Deepling, and after killing it… most of the time you get nothing. I thought: hey, wouldn’t it be cool if you could at least get some meat from it? That’s how it all started, and the other food items kinda showed up like a domino effect after that. - Also, it always felt a bit disappointing that bosses didn’t drop any type of food — so now, yeah, you’ve got meals from bosses too, with some strong effects packed in.
